title: Add alpha content catalog and validation fixtures
summary: Add the first M5 alpha content catalog definitions and validation fixtures for buildings, tags, localization references and review notes without bespoke runtime code per item.
acceptance: Alpha content catalog provides 20 to 30 building/tag/localization-ready definitions that map to approved owner surfaces or explicitly block
Every catalog entry passes M5 schema reference semantic localization and deterministic compile-order validation
Catalog entries reuse public rule components and do not require bespoke runtime code per item
Design review notes record system value alternatives faction/governance/anomaly hooks and culture/fairness review needs where applicable
Validation fixtures include accepted and rejected examples for buildings tags anomalies faction hooks season events localization and unsafe data

history.details: Created by WM-0072 M5 planning DAG; remains proposed until dependencies are done.
Added WM-0079 alpha catalog fixtures and validation coverage; all required gates pass
changes_requested: High: accepted catalog entries cite unapproved/nonexistent owner surfaces instead of reviewed owner surfaces or explicit blocked rows; tests only check ownerSurfaces is non-empty.
Repaired owner-surface allowlist for WM-0079 catalog entries; all gates pass
verified: No blocking findings; owner-surface allowlist repair verified, blocked catalog row preserved, and WM-0079 validation gates pass.
Gate verified WM-0079; full integration gate passed with 57 unit files / 329 tests

objective: Create the first validated alpha content catalog and fixture set for downstream integration.
risksAndFileOwnership: Risk: content volume outruns schema; every entry must pass validation and cite owner surfaces.
Risk: content requires bespoke code; entries must block instead of adding runtime hacks.
Risk: cultural/fairness issues hide in catalog text; review notes must name required checks.
benchmarkImpact: Adds content count validation artifact and fixture metrics only; no runtime baseline update until WM-0083.
reviewRouting: Content-worker completes to reviewer; gameplay-designer and systems-architect should be consulted for content value and schema/ownership blockers.
rollbackModel: Revert alpha content definitions fixtures artifacts docs and report; leave schema/runtime owner tasks intact.
ownerGate: No bespoke runtime code per content item
Every accepted entry maps to approved owner surfaces
Block on unsafe content public contract dependency or M6 scope

sparkEligibility.reason: Although content-heavy, the first alpha catalog validates new schema and ownership policy and should not be assigned to Spark.

review.summary: No blocking findings; owner-surface allowlist repair verified, blocked catalog row preserved, and WM-0079 validation gates pass.

integration.summary: Gate verified WM-0079; full integration gate passed with 57 unit files / 329 tests
